skills.title: Frontend Design
skills.description: Create clean, functional interfaces with optimal user experience through modular component design and performance-focused implementation.
guidelines: Keep components small, focused, and adhering to single responsibility principle
Use React hooks appropriately, avoid prop drilling, leverage context for global state
Implement TypeScript for robust type checking
Use React.memo(), useMemo(), useCallback(), code splitting with React.lazy()
Use functional components with hooks over class components
Context API for infrequently changing global state
Avoid creating overly complex abstractions

skills.title: Web Artifacts Builder
skills.description: Create reusable, maintainable web projects through disciplined architectural principles and modular design patterns.
guidelines: Use clear directory structure (models/, services/, controllers/, utils/, config/, tests/)
Ensure each file has one clear purpose and single responsibility
Separate business logic from controllers/routes using service classes
Adopt domain-driven design approach for complex systems
Implement comprehensive testing for critical workflows
Avoid mixing business logic with presentation/route handling
Use thin controllers focused on orchestration

skills.title: MCP Builder
skills.description: Implement robust object-oriented design patterns with clear separation between data models, business logic, and presentation layers.
guidelines: Prefer object-oriented programming (OOP) over procedural programming
Bundle behavior with data through classes for proper encapsulation
Favor composition over inheritance for code reuse
Use domain objects representing real concepts rather than raw data
Keep classes under 150 lines, functions under 40 lines
Refactor when signs of complexity appear
Avoid deep inheritance hierarchies (max 2 levels)

skills.title: Skill Creator
skills.description: Design and implement reusable, maintainable skills/components through disciplined coding practices and AI-assisted development.
guidelines: Modify existing structures instead of rewriting large sections
Maintain established patterns and architectural conventions
Break complex features into smaller classes and services
Follow naming conventions (PascalCase for classes, snake_case for functions)
Use clear, intention-revealing names for all code elements
Prioritize readability above all other coding considerations
Implement structured error handling throughout the codebase

skills.title: Object-Oriented Programming
skills.description: Organize related logic into classes instead of loose functions, encapsulate behavior with data, and keep classes focused on single responsibility.
guidelines: Organize related logic into classes instead of loose functions
Encapsulate behavior with the data it operates on
Keep classes focused on a single responsibility
Prefer composition over inheritance
Use inheritance only when there's a true 'is-a' relationship
Build small collaborating objects instead of large procedural scripts
Use domain models, service classes, and value objects appropriately

skills.title: Cryptographic Security
skills.description: Implement cryptographic operations securely with misuse-resistant APIs and rigorous input validation.
guidelines: Use HMAC-based key derivation functions (HKDF) with unique info values
Never reuse key/IV pairs - generate fresh random IVs for each encryption
Use memory-hard KDFs like Argon2 instead of fast hash functions
Implement authenticated encryption modes (AES-GCM) with mandatory tag verification
Validate cryptographic inputs rigorously - reject ambiguous/null values
Use per-entry key derivation for database encryption
Avoid dangerous defaults in cryptographic APIs

skills.title: Static Vulnerability Analysis
skills.description: Use automated static analysis tools like CodeQL and Semgrep for comprehensive vulnerability detection with proper false positive verification.
guidelines: Implement systematic false positive verification before reporting findings
Use Semgrep rule variant analysis to find similar vulnerabilities
Scan for insecure defaults, hardcoded credentials, and fail-open patterns
Integrate supply chain risk assessment into dependency management
Perform differential code review with git history analysis
Create custom detection rules for domain-specific vulnerability patterns
Use property-based testing to discover edge cases

skills.title: Secure API Design
skills.description: Design APIs that make secure usage the path of least resistance through semantic typing and explicit error handling.
guidelines: Design semantic APIs with meaningful types instead of raw bytes/strings
Make secure configurations the default and require explicit opt-out
Use type safety to prevent incorrect parameter ordering
Provide clear, explicit error messages for security failures
Audit GitHub Actions workflows for AI agent security vulnerabilities
Implement error-prone API detection for dangerous configurations
Use context-aware entry point analysis for complex systems

skills.title: Security Development Workflow
skills.description: Integrate security considerations throughout the development lifecycle using automated scanning and systematic review processes.
guidelines: Ask clarifying questions when requirements are underspecified
Use iterative refinement with automated feedback loops
Implement supply chain threat landscape auditing
Use automated scanners for vulnerabilities across platforms
Create systematic documentation of rationalizations to reject
Apply architectural review with system context understanding
Use differential analysis focused on security implications

skills.title: File Size & Complexity Management
skills.description: Maintain readable and maintainable code by enforcing size limits and recognizing refactoring triggers.
guidelines: Keep files under 300 lines, classes under 150 lines, functions under 40 lines
Refactor when functions exceed ~40 lines
Refactor when classes handle multiple responsibilities
Refactor when duplicate logic appears in multiple places
Refactor when more than 3 nested conditionals are present
Use extract method, extract class, introduce service object techniques
Use introduce value object pattern for immutable data structures

skills.title: Function Design Principles
skills.description: Ensure functions are small, predictable, single-purpose, and side-effect aware for better maintainability.
guidelines: Keep functions small, focused, and concise
Ensure functions have predictable and consistent behavior
Design functions with single, clear responsibility
Be mindful of side effects and dependencies
Avoid functions that try to do everything
Prefer simple, focused functions like user_service.create_user()
Avoid complex, multi-purpose functions like do_everything_and_save_user()

skills.title: React Component Design
skills.description: Build React components that are small, focused, type-safe, and optimized for performance.
guidelines: Keep components small and focused on single responsibility
Use functional components with hooks instead of class components
Implement TypeScript for robust type safety
Avoid prop drilling - use context for shared state
Use useState for local component state management
Use useContext for global state that doesn't change often
Consider Redux/Zustand/Jotai for complex application state

skills.title: React Performance Optimization
skills.description: Optimize React applications through proper memoization, code splitting, and re-render prevention techniques.
guidelines: Use React.memo() for expensive components to prevent unnecessary re-renders
Use useMemo() and useCallback() appropriately with proper dependency arrays
Implement code splitting with React.lazy() for better initial load times
Avoid unnecessary state lifting that causes excessive re-renders
Use proper dependency arrays to optimize effect execution
Consider virtual scrolling for large lists and data sets
Profile and optimize render performance with React DevTools

skills.title: Error Handling Philosophy
skills.description: Implement clear, structured error handling that communicates issues effectively without silently swallowing problems.
guidelines: Fail loudly and clearly so issues are immediately apparent
Do not silently swallow exceptions or errors
Provide specific, useful error messages for debugging
Use structured exception handling patterns consistently
Document error conditions and expected recovery paths
Validate inputs before processing to prevent predictable errors
Log errors appropriately for monitoring and debugging

skills.title: Readability Priority System
skills.description: Prioritize code readability above all else, followed by simplicity, maintainability, and performance.
guidelines: Prioritize readability as the most important code quality
Ensure code is simple and straightforward
Focus on maintainability for long-term projects
Optimize for performance only when necessary
Aim for code that new developers can understand quickly
Use clear variable and function names that reveal intent
Structure code with logical flow and minimal mental overhead
